basic_details.job_title: GSSSB X-Ray Technician Class 3 Recruitment 2026: 246 Vacancies, Apply Online by 29 June
basic_details.short_information: The Gujarat Subordinate Service Selection Board (GSSSB) has officially released 246 vacancies for the post of X-Ray Technician, Class 3 under the Health and Family Welfare Department. Online applications open on 15 June 2026 and close on 29 June 2026 via the OJAS portal at ojas.gujarat.gov.in. The exam fee deadline is 1 July 2026. Candidates must hold a B.Sc. degree and have completed a recognised X-Ray Technician course to be eligible.

application_fee.details: The exam fee must be paid online only - no other payment mode is accepted. Candidates who appear for the exam are eligible for a refund; those who do not appear forfeit their fee. | Payment Methods: Debit Card, Internet Banking, UPI, or Digital Wallet. | After paying, candidates must download and preserve the payment receipt. If no receipt is generated, the fee is not considered paid and the application remains invalid.

qualification.details: Candidates should confirm all eligibility conditions as of 29 June 2026 - the official cut-off date for age, qualifications, and certificate validity. | To apply for the GSSSB X-Ray Technician post, candidates must satisfy all three of the following conditions: | A Bachelor's degree in Science from a University established under any Central or State Act in India, or an institution declared a Deemed University under Section 3 of the UGC Act, 1956. Completion of the X-Ray Technician course conducted by a Government Institute or a Medical College recognised by the National Medical Council. Basic computer knowledge as prescribed under Gujarat Civil Services Classification (General) Rules, 1967. Adequate knowledge of Gujarati or Hindi or both. | Candidates who do not yet hold a computer proficiency certificate may still apply, but must produce the certificate before appointment. Failure to do so will result in cancellation of selection. | Ex-Servicemen receive an additional relaxation equal to the length of military service rendered, plus 3 years, subject to a ceiling of 45 years. | Candidates already serving in Gujarat Government and belonging to reserved categories receive age relaxation as per Gujarat Civil Services Classification and Recruitment (General) Rules, 1967. | Candidates with the following disabilities are eligible to apply: | Group A (Visual): Low Vision (40–70%) Group B (Hearing): Deaf, Hard of Hearing Group C (Locomotor): One Leg, Leprosy Cured, Dwarfism, Acid Attack Victim, Spinal Deformity, Spinal Injury (without neurological/limb dysfunction) Group D & E: Autism Spectrum Disorder (Mild), Specific Learning Disability (40–70%), Mental Illness (40–70%), Multiple Disabilities | DON'T MISSSSC CGL 12256 Group B & Group C Online Form 202612,256 Posts • Group B & Group C • SSC22 Jun 202612,256 postsRRB 6565 Technician Online Form 2026 (Short Notice)6,565 Vacancies • Technician29 Jul 20266,565 postsOSSSC 5989 Nursing Officer Online Form 20265,989 Openings • OSSSC13 Jul 20265,989 postsGSRTC 4599 Driver Online Form 20264,599 Posts • GSRTC05 Jul 20264,599 postsCentral Bank of India 4,500 Apprentice Recruitment 20264,500 Vacancies • Apprentice • Central Bank of India22 Jun 20264,500 postsGSRTC 4318 Conductor Online Form 20264,318 Openings • Conductor05 Jul 20264,318 posts

salary_details.details: Selected candidates will initially be appointed on a fixed pay basis for the first five years, after which - subject to satisfactory service - they will be regularised on the 7th Pay Commission scale. | The transition from fixed to regular pay is subject to the Supreme Court's ruling in SLP No. 14124/2012 and SLP No. 14125/2012.

selection_process: Part A Reasoning & Data Interpretation, Quantitative Aptitude, Indian Constitution, Current Affairs, Gujarati & English Comprehension 90 90
Part B X-Ray Technician Technical Subject 120 120
GSSSB will select candidates through a single-stage competitive written examination followed by document verification. There is no interview round.
The exam is divided into two parts:

Total Duration: 3 hours (180 minutes) for both parts combined. Negative Marking: ¼ mark deducted per wrong answer. Question paper level: Commensurate with the required educational qualification. Languages: Part A questions in Gujarati and English; Gujarati comprehension in Gujarati only; English comprehension in English only. Part B in both Gujarati and English.
Minimum qualifying marks: 40% in Part A and 40% in Part B - independently. No relaxation below 40% for any category.
Candidates ranked approximately twice the number of vacancies (category-wise) in the provisional merit list will be called for document verification.
The final merit list and waiting list are prepared based on marks obtained
Widows: 5% bonus marks added to MCQ/CBRT score (subject to proof of non-remarriage at appointment). Recognised Sports Persons (National/International
or AISSE level): 5% bonus marks added to MCQ/CBRT score (subject to valid certificate from competent authority).

syllabus.details: Candidates appearing for the technical section should prepare the following topics:

how_to_apply: The GSSSB X-Ray Technician 2026 online application process is entirely paperless. Here is the complete process:
Click on "Online Application" → Apply → select GSSSB
Find Advertisement No. 424/202627 (X-Ray Technician, Class 3) and click Apply Now
Click Skip to open the Application Format; fill in all Personal Details (mandatory fields marked with a red asterisk *)
Accept the Assurance (Baandhedari) terms and click Save - an Application Number will be generated; note it carefully

Upload your photograph (5 cm height × 3.6 cm width, JPG, max 15 KB) and signature (2.5 cm height × 7.5 cm width, JPG, max 15 KB) Photo must have a white background with the date of clicking visible; date must not be more than 1 year before the last application date
Photo must have a white background with the date of clicking visible; date must not be more than 1 year before the last application date
Click Confirm Application, verify all details, then confirm - a Confirmation Number is generated; this is required for all future correspondence
Download and print the application form - keep it until the entire recruitment process is over
Pay the exam fee online via Print Application Form / Pay Fees → Online Payment of Fees using Debit Card, Internet Banking, UPI, or Wallet - by 1 July 2026 (23:59 hrs)
Download and preserve the fee payment receipt

Important: Once confirmed, no changes can be made to the application - not by the candidate, not by GSSSB. Category (caste) entered at confirmation is final. If correction is needed, submit a fresh application with a new Confirmation Number and pay the fee again before the deadline.

all_official_links.label: Download GSSSB X Ray Technician, Class-3 Job 2026 Notification PDF

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for GSSSB X-Ray Technician 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application is 29 June 2026 by 23:59 hours. The deadline to pay the exam fee is 1 July 2026 by 23:59 hours.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available in GSSSB X-Ray Technician Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 246 vacancies are available for the post of X-Ray Technician, Class 3, distributed across General, EWS, SEBC, SC, and ST categories.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required?
faq.answer: Candidates must hold a B.Sc. degree from a recognised university and must have completed an X-Ray Technician course from a Government Institute or NMC-recognised Medical College. Basic computer knowledge is also mandatory.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for GSSSB X-Ray Technician 2026?
faq.answer: The general age limit is 18 to 36 years as on 29 June 2026. Age relaxation is available - up to 41 years for General women and reserved category men, up to 45 years for reserved category women and Divyang candidates.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee?
faq.answer: The fee is ₹500 for General/Unreserved candidates and ₹400 for reserved category candidates (including all women, SEBC, SC, ST, EWS, Divyang, and Ex-Servicemen). Payment is accepted online only.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for this recruitment?
faq.answer: Selection is through a single written competitive exam (MCQ-based, CBRT/OMR format) consisting of 210 questions (90 in Part A + 120 in Part B) over 3 hours, followed by document verification. There is no interview.

faq.question: Q7. Is there negative marking in the exam?
faq.answer: Yes. ¼ mark is deducted for every wrong answer. Questions left unanswered do not attract any penalty.

faq.question: Q8. What is the salary for GSSSB X-Ray Technician?
faq.answer: For the first five years, the fixed pay is ₹40,800 per month. After satisfactory completion of five years, candidates are regularised at the 7th CPC pay scale of ₹35,400 to ₹1,12,400 (Level 6).

faq.question: Q9. Where can I apply for GSSSB X-Ray Technician 2026?
faq.answer: Applications must be submitted online at https://ojas.gujarat.gov.in only. No offline or postal applications are accepted.

faq.question: Q10. Can I make changes to my application after confirmation?
faq.answer: No. Once an application is confirmed, no changes - including to category/caste - can be made by either the candidate or GSSSB. If correction is needed before the deadline, submit a fresh application and pay the fee again.

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for GSSSB X-Ray Technician 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application is 29 June 2026 by 23:59 hours. The deadline to pay the exam fee is 1 July 2026 by 23:59 hours.
